# Sources — Phoenix Platform

> Verbatim source text appended chronologically. One file per project.

## 2026-03-25 — meeting: Phoenix Platform Weekly

> Marcus walked the team through the ingestion path state. Surfaced the validator dependency — infra team's validator service is failing under load and we can't move forward without it. Agreed to ping them again and start a fallback design if there's no response by April 8.

Referenced by: [[phoenix-platform]] — Timeline (blocker callout), Open Tasks (Marcus fallback design delegation)

## 2026-04-05 — slack: #phoenix-eng, Marcus Walker

> Still nothing from infra on the validator. That's 14 days. I'm starting the fallback design today regardless — we can't keep waiting. Sam, do you want to escalate or should I keep pinging?

Referenced by: [[phoenix-platform]] — Timeline (escalation needed callout), Open Tasks (escalation task)

## 2026-04-07 — meeting: Phoenix Blocker Review

> Alex walked through the infra team response pattern. Marcus frustrated, said his project is "on ice." Talked through options: (1) keep pinging, (2) formal escalation through James, (3) start fallback design. Marcus initially resistant to fallback — felt like giving up. Reframed as: fallback is forward motion, escalation is long-term fix, do both. Marcus landed on it.

Referenced by: [[phoenix-platform]] — Timeline (parallel path decision), [[marcus-walker]] — Observations

## 2026-04-10 — meeting: 1:1 with Marcus Walker

> Marcus brought the written fallback design. Clean structure. Realistic estimates. Called out his own risks. Asked him about the blocker review meeting — he said he "froze" when the scope cut came up. Talked about why: felt like his failure. Reframed scope cuts as normal engineering. Landed well. Parental leave filed, start May 18 for 12 weeks. HR has a process.

Referenced by: [[phoenix-platform]] — Timeline, [[marcus-walker]] — Observations (strength: written design)
